title: Futago
description: A game created at the 10th ImoGameJam
detailedFeatures: The jam theme was "Link", so our team discussed and decided to make a game where you link enemies to deal damage and defeat them in chains. It took me a while to understand the damage-linking specifications from our planners, but the core game loop was completed successfully. Making the UI and in-game explanations easier for players to understand is something to improve on.
myResponsibilities: Player, enemies, damage calculation, sound playback, game loop, etc.

title: Virtual Link Battle
description: A VTuber battle game created on campus. First long-term, large-scale project.
detailedFeatures: A game where VTubers battle on a live streaming stage. The main focus was on comments. Initially, I used TMP to scroll text, but it lacked depth and didn't utilize the 3D space effectively. So I created a Python tool to generate 3D models from input text, executed it in Blender to create them in batch, and replaced the system to play them back. The result looked much better. I also created special comments and gimmicks that only work because of live streaming. At the time, I was still inexperienced with Unity, so I struggled with 3D model display and rendering.

title: Teru Teru Wars
detailedFeatures: Inversion → flip or turn over → started creating game ideas from 'When you flip a teru teru bozu (Japanese weather doll), it changes from sunny to rainy!' We decided to make a tower defense game where the effects change by flipping teru teru bozu!
myResponsibilities: Sound-related classes, scene transition classes, AI-generated images and sounds

title: Assassination Reversal
description: A work created at the UnityRoom game jam held in March 2026
detailedFeatures: From the theme 'ura' (back/reverse), we created 'a game where you flip tiles, use gimmicks, and get behind enemies to assassinate them.'

title: The Mask Ball Seeker
description: A work created at Global Game Jam held in January 2026
detailedFeatures: From the theme 'MASK', after discussing with team members, we decided to create 'a game where you solve a murder case at a masquerade ball!' Everyone was meeting for the first time and I was nervous at first, but we were able to complete it!
myResponsibilities: Player design and implementation, NPC implementation, some mini-game implementation, sound and overall scene management class creation

title: MoreHit2
description: A work created for the UnityRoom game jam held in December 2025
detailedFeatures: A shooting game developed over 1 week at a UnityRoom game jam. I focused on difficulty balancing for enemies and bosses, gradually introducing new enemy types and culminating in a boss battle. I also paid special attention to UI and sound effects to create a sense of urgency for players.
myResponsibilities: Player design and implementation, enemy base class and boss implementation, sound and overall scene management class creation

title: This Side / That Side of the Line
description: A work created in a game jam hosted by HIBIGA
detailedFeatures: An action game developed over 2 days at a HIBIGA-hosted game jam. I utilized the 'line' theme to create unique gameplay where players switch between above and below a line. Within the limited timeframe, I carefully designed the overall game state transitions and sound management to create a polished final product.
myResponsibilities: Created sound class, implemented game overall state transitions, created some base classes

title: TofuRunner
description: First work published on the App Store
detailedFeatures: A game created together with people from school. Also used Shader for the first time to achieve more realistic rendering.

title: Shin-layer Shin-ri Mine-chan
description: A 2-week game jam entry for Bandai Namco Studios Cup
detailedFeatures: This game was created collaboratively with professionals from various fields including designers, planners, sound engineers, and many other disciplines beyond programming.
myResponsibilities: Stage design and implementation, implementation of several boss skills

title: Takashi, Betting on Life
description: 2D shooting game developed in 3 days during school game jam. Led a team of 5. Won Horikawa Award from Infinite Loop Inc.
detailedFeatures:
This game was created during a 3-day in-school game jam. As the project leader for my first game jam, managing the team was challenging.
It was a great learning experience coordinating with designers on the type of artwork needed and the format. With many programmers on the team, creating base classes and shortening the critical path was quite difficult.
I was just starting to learn design patterns, so I worked hard on optimization using Singleton, ObjectPool, and other patterns.
myResponsibilities: Project Leader, Base Class Design, Singleton Pattern Implementation, ObjectPool System Implementation
award: Horikawa Award from Infinite Loop Inc.

title: Butterfly Rebellion
description: Sapporo Game Camp 2025 entry. 2D action game developed with a team of 7, all meeting for the first time. First external game jam challenge.
detailedFeatures:
This game was created for Sapporo Game Camp 2025. Everyone was meeting for the first time, and it was my first time participating in an external game jam.
Applying lessons from the previous in-school game jam, I prepared by creating the Unity project and base classes in advance, and setting up repository integration.
While we successfully completed the game, we couldn't prepare all the planned stages, and I regret not being able to use all the artwork created by the designers.
myResponsibilities: Base class design, player bullet implementation, enemy behavior and attack pattern implementation
teamSize: 7 people (1 Professional Programmer, 2 Student Programmers, 1 Professional Designer, 2 Student Designers, 1 Student Planner)

title: Git Command Helper
description: Command-focused website for Git learning. Practical learning tool.
detailedFeatures: A tool I created so I could quickly find commands when I forgot them and study more efficiently. Developed as a website for efficiently learning and referencing Git commands.

title: UnderOver
description: 2D action game developed while learning Unity-specific methods. Culmination of Unity basics.
detailedFeatures: A game I made while reviewing Unity's unique methods. Through 2D action game development, I solidified my understanding of Unity's basic features and game creation fundamentals.

title: ElementBattle
description: My memorable first work - a card game. Developed while learning functions and variables. A memorable work where I learned programming basics.
detailedFeatures: This is the first game I made by myself. I created it while learning functions, variables, and other basics. It's a very memorable work where I learned fundamental programming concepts through actual game development.

title: SPLIT
description: 2D action game created in first team development. Experienced full process from planning to debugging. First Git experience.
detailedFeatures: I experienced everything from planning to development and debugging for the first time. I remember the chaos when I first encountered Git. This was a valuable experience learning team development basics and realizing the importance of version control systems.
